precedents.title: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala (1973)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Supreme Court ne kaha ki Parliament Constitution badal sakti hai, lekin uske 'Basic Structure' ko touch nahi kar sakti.
precedents.professional_analysis: Established the 'Basic Structure Doctrine', limiting Parliament's power to amend the Constitution under Article 368.

precedents.title: Association for Democratic Reforms v. Union of India (2024)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Electoral Bonds ko unconstitutional bola gaya kyunki voters ko funding ke source ka pata hona chahiye.
precedents.professional_analysis: Struck down the Electoral Bonds Scheme for violating Article 19(1)(a) by allowing anonymous political funding.

precedents.title: Supriyo v. Union of India (2023)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Court ne legal marriage recognize nahi ki, par discrimination ke khilaf kadak rules banaye.
precedents.professional_analysis: Declined to recognize same-sex marriage legally, leaving it to Parliament, while emphasizing the right to a union.

precedents.title: Bilkis Yakub Rasool v. Union of India (2024)
precedents.simplified_explanation: 11 convicts ki rihayi cancel hui kyunki Gujarat govt ke paas jurisdiction nahi tha.
precedents.professional_analysis: Quashed the remission granted to convicts, ruling that the state where the trial occurred has the power to remit.

precedents.title: Justice K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India (2017)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Privacy har Bharatiya ka ek Fundamental Right hai.
precedents.professional_analysis: Upheld the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right protected under Article 21 and Part III.

precedents.title: State of Punjab v. Davinder Singh (2024)
precedents.simplified_explanation: States ab SC/ST categories ke beech sub-groups bana sakte hain sacha fayda dene ke liye.
precedents.professional_analysis: Permitted sub-classification within Reserved Categories to ensure benefits reach the most marginalized sub-groups.

precedents.title: X v. Principal Secretary, Health (2022)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Unmarried women ko bhi 24 weeks tak abortion ka pura haq hai.
precedents.professional_analysis: Extended abortion rights to unmarried women up to 24 weeks, ensuring equality in reproductive choices.

precedents.title: SITA v. State of Jharkhand (2024)
precedents.simplified_explanation: MPs aur MLAs ko rishwat (bribe) lene par assembly mein immunity nahi milegi.
precedents.professional_analysis: Overruled the 1998 PV Narasimha Rao judgment; held that legislators do not enjoy immunity for taking bribes related to speeches or votes.

precedents.title: Common Cause v. Union of India (2023 Update)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Dignified death aur 'Living Will' ke rules ko easy bana diya gaya.
precedents.professional_analysis: Simplified the procedure for 'Passive Euthanasia' and the execution of Advanced Medical Directives (Living Wills).

precedents.title: Kaushal Kishor v. State of Uttar Pradesh (2023)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Ministers aur bade officials ke speech par extra restrictions nahi lagaye ja sakte, bas jo law mein hain wahi.
precedents.professional_analysis: Held that additional restrictions cannot be imposed on the free speech of public functionaries beyond Article 19(2).

bns_mappings.simplified: Hatya ki dhara ab 103 hai.
Hatya ki koshish ab 109 hai.
Dhokhadhari ab 318 mein aayegi.
Rape ki saza ab Section 64 mein hai.
Chori ab Section 303 mein hai.
Patni par zulm ab Section 85 mein hai.
Maan-haani ab Section 356 mein hai.
Dhamki dena ab Section 351 mein hai.
Dakaiti ab Section 310 mein hai.
Bharosa todna (Breach of trust) ab 316 mein hai.
Saazish rachna ab 61 mein hai.
Desh-droh (Integrity) ab Section 152 mein hai.
Gair-kanooni bheed ab Section 189 mein hai.
Chot pahunchana ab Section 115 mein hai.
Mahila ki maryada se khelna ab Section 74 mein hai.
Apaharan (Kidnapping) ab Section 137 mein hai.
Bina ijazat ghusna ab Section 329 mein hai.
Nakli documents banana ab 336 mein hai.

bns_mappings.change: Specific BNS clause for group-based murder.
bns_mappings.simplified: Mob lynching ki hatya ab 103(2) mein hogi.

bns_mappings.offense: Sex on false promise of marriage
bns_mappings.simplified: Jhuthe shaadi ke waade par relation banana ab Section 69 mein hai.

glossary.meaning: The objective legal reason for a court's decision.
Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita - Successor to IPC.
Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
